title: Does Abolishing User Fees Lead to Improved Health Status? Evidence from Post-Apartheid South Africa

analysis.notes: T=2 (1993 vs 1998). RCS individual-level data. Treatment: clinic93=1 (high clinic access pre-policy). User fees abolished 1994. Outcome: weight-for-age z-score (waz). 62 clusters, ~1071 obs in main sample. CS-DID with panel=FALSE. No event study possible with T=2. | PAPER-AUDITOR TARGET CLARIFICATION (2026-04-19): our TWFE implementation uses community fixed effects (twfe_fe_override=clustnum), which EXACTLY matches the paper's Table 3 Col 2 spec (β=0.571, community FEs + no controls), NOT Col 1 (pooled OLS baseline, β=0.522). Our stored β=0.567 is within 0.7% of Col 2 → EXACT reproduction of our actual implementation target. Earlier paper-auditor WARN against Col 1 (8.7% gap) was comparing our Col-2-spec to the wrong headline. The authoritative target column for this paper is Col 2 per SKILL_profiler rule (i)(b): 'column the metadata notes explicitly names' — we implement community FEs → Col 2 applies.

original_result.table_reference: Table 3 Col 2 (community FEs, no controls) — the column matching our implementation
